taxonomy: Kozola japonica is classified within the family Ebenaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants native to Japan. This family is part of the order Ebenales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in East Asia. The order Ebenales is further grouped into the subclass Dilleniidae, a subclass of flowering plants that also includes other families such as the Actinidiaceae and the Clethraceae. The subclass Dilleniidae is part of the class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledonous flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants in East Asia.

Its medicinal properties are attributed to the plant's ability to lower fever and alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold.
